title: Nursery Rhyme. CCLXVI. Gaffers And Gammers.
content:
[The first two lines of the following are the same with those of a song in D'Urfey's 'Pills to Purge Melancholy,' vol. v, p. 13.]

        There was an old woman
            Lived under a hill,
        She put a mouse in a bag,
            And sent it to mill;

        The miller declar'd
            By the point of his knife,
        He never took toll
            Of a mouse in his life.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Unknown, titled "Nursery Rhyme. CCLXVI. Gaffers And Gammers.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
